title: Did the method change my marks?

mcq.explanation_md: Java passes a **copy** of the value into the method. Inside `addGrace`, the parameter `marks` is a separate box that starts at 40 and becomes 50 — then vanishes when the method returns. The `marks` in `main` never moves, so it prints `40`. The tempting answer is `50`, assuming the method reaches back and edits the caller's variable — it can't; Java is pass-by-value, always. To actually apply grace marks, return the new value and assign it: `marks = addGrace(marks);`.
